summary: At 08:01:50 UTC the application pod was killed by the Linux OOM killer after memory usage reached 96% of the 512MB container limit. On restart the app could not reconnect to PostgreSQL (connection timeout), causing the supervisor to exhaust its 3 restart attempts. With all backend pods down, Nginx returned 502 Bad Gateway to all requests. A Kubernetes HPA attempted to scale up but hit the pod quota (10/10), preventing recovery.
root_cause:
Primary: Memory leak / unbounded cache growth in the application. The app attempted to load the full product catalog into an in-memory cache (OutOfMemoryError: Failed to allocate 64MB for product catalog cache), exhausting the 512MB container memory limit. Contributing: PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion (max connections hit at 08:01:30) meant that when the app restarted it could not obtain a DB connection within 5 s, preventing any successful restart. Compounding: Kubernetes pod quota was set equal to the current running count, so HPA-triggered scale-out was entirely blocked.

⚠️  GUARDRAIL WARNING: One or more statements in this root cause analysis could not be fully verified against the log evidence. Please manually review before publishing.
immediate_fixes: kubectl rollout restart deployment/app  # force fresh pod with clean memory
psql -c 'SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = \'idle\' AND query_start < now() - interval \'5 min\';'
# Scale deployment manually past quota once quota is raised:
permanent_fixes: Implement LRU/TTL-bounded cache for product catalog (e.g., limit to 100MB max)
Add JVM/runtime heap limits aligned with container memory limit minus OS overhead
Set Kubernetes memory requests = limits for Guaranteed QoS (prevents OOM priority loss)
Add PgBouncer connection pooler to decouple app connection count from PostgreSQL max_connections
Raise pod quota to 2× expected max replicas; add buffer for HPA burst
Add liveness probe with /memory endpoint to restart pod before OOM kill
Add readiness probe back-off so nginx removes pod from rotation before it is unresponsive
Implement Alertmanager alert: memory > 80% for > 2 minutes → page on-call

claims_checked.text: No memory limit headroom — container limit (512MB) set too c
No circuit breaker on the product catalog cache load
PostgreSQL max_connections exhausted before OOM kill — resta
Pod quota (10) matched running pods (10) — HPA scale-out imp
No PodDisruptionBudget — all replicas could be OOM-killed si

claims_removed: No memory limit headroom — container limit (512MB) set too close to normal working set
No circuit breaker on the product catalog cache load
PostgreSQL max_connections exhausted before OOM kill — restart was doomed
Pod quota (10) matched running pods (10) — HPA scale-out impossible
No PodDisruptionBudget — all replicas could be OOM-killed simultaneously
[timeline] Supervisor: max restarts (3) reached — pod dead
